steps.title: Distinguish shift from expansion
steps.content: A simple southward shift would make Peru wet but Central America dry. An expansion northward and southward would allow both regions to show high rainfall during the Little Ice Age.

steps.title: Look for shared rainfall
steps.content: Choice A does not address rainfall. Choice B describes dry conditions, not expansion-related wetness. Choice C gives the wet-Peru, dry-Central-America pattern that caused the original problem. The needed finding shows both cave records moving together with high rainfall. The answer is D.
